hero.title: Dr. Asiia Vinograd
hero.subtitle: MD, PhD
hero.desc: Preparing for USMLE Step 1 together — high-yield lectures, clinical cases and study materials for international medical graduates.

about.title: Credentials & Experience
about.p1: I am a physician, cardiologist, and researcher currently based in Phuket, Thailand — preparing for USMLE Step 1 alongside my students.
about.p2: My path includes medical school at Bashkir State Medical University, cardiology residency, and a PhD from Harbin Medical University in China. I also have 2.5 years of NICU nursing experience, which shaped my passion for neonatology and pediatrics.

problem.title: The struggles every IMG knows
problem.subtitle: Most Russian-speaking IMGs face the same walls when preparing for USMLE.

chaos.title: Too many resources, zero system
chaos.desc: First Aid, Pathoma, Sketchy, UWorld. You switch all day and feel like you're going nowhere.

language.title: Language barrier slows you down
language.desc: You understand the medicine — but English explanations take twice as long to process.

noSystem.title: No idea where to start
noSystem.desc: Everyone says 'just do UWorld' but nobody tells you what to read first, in what order, for how long.

noProgress.title: No sense of progress
noProgress.desc: You study for weeks and still can't tell if you're actually ready for the exam.

format.title: USMLE format feels foreign
format.desc: Two-step clinical vignettes are nothing like the exams you took back home.

alone.title: Studying alone is exhausting
alone.desc: No community, no one to ask, no accountability. Just you and a pile of PDFs.

problem.cta: Dr. Vinograd went through this — and built a system that works.

pricing.title: Choose your path
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Study where it's comfortable for you — Telegram, YouTube, or through the website.
pricing.note: No lock-in. Cancel or switch anytime.

plans.name: Free Access
plans.desc: Everything you need to get started with USMLE prep right now.
features: Telegram channel with updates and case breakdowns
Study guides and free lecture notes

plans.name: USMLE Club
plans.desc: Full access to all study materials and personal support from Dr. Asiia Vinograd.

items.a: For international medical graduates (IMGs) from Russia, Kazakhstan, Ukraine, and other CIS countries preparing for USMLE Step 1. Whether you are just starting or already studying — Dr. Vinograd's content is designed for you.

items.q: Do I need to speak English well to study here?
items.a: No. All lectures are available in both Russian and English. You can start in Russian and gradually switch to English — which is a great strategy for learning USMLE terminology naturally.

items.q: What is the difference between free and paid access?
items.a: Free access includes YouTube lectures, the Telegram channel with updates and case breakdowns, and free lecture notes. Paid access (USMLE Club) adds Anki decks, PDF notes, clinical case breakdowns, and a personal consultation call with Dr. Vinograd. You can also subscribe directly through the website.

items.q: Can I pay in rubles or other currencies?
items.a: Yes — all payment methods are available on the website, including card payments in rubles and other currencies.

items.q: Is Dr. Vinograd currently preparing for USMLE herself?
items.a: Yes — she is actively preparing for Step 1 alongside her students. This means the content is current, practical, and built around what actually matters for the exam right now.

All high-yield Step 1 disciplines: Microbiology, Pharmacology, Cardiology, Pathology, GI & Liver, Immunology, Nephrology & Pulmonology, Neurology & Psychiatry — with clinical vignettes and diagnostic algorithms.
Start by watching the free YouTube lectures and joining the Telegram channel. When you are ready for more — subscribe to USMLE Club through the website or Patreon.
